chunks.text: May 12, 2023: The 9th Annual Berkeley-Stanford Women in EECS research meetup took place on Saturday, April 29th in the Wozniak Lounge, Soda Hall. The meetup offers all female-identifying, transgender, non-binary, and gender non-conforming graduate students a chance to learn about each other’s research and to network with alums from industry and academia.
chunks.question: What event celebrated the 45th anniversary of WiCSE?

chunks.text: April 27, 2023: The College of Engineering held a groundbreaking ceremony for its new Engineering Center on April 21. The new building, scheduled for completion in 2025, will be a hub for student collaboration, innovation, and entrepreneurship, serving as a space for students across different disciplines and perspectives.
chunks.question: What will the new Engineering Center at Berkeley serve as?
chunks.answer: A hub for student collaboration and innovation.

chunks.text: March 30, 2023: In honor of Women’s History Month, Berkeley EECS and BART worked together to Embrace Equity in STEM. Over 50 years, both organizations have strived to be engines of societal change and social mobility, continuing today with BART providing fast, reliable transportation.
chunks.question: What did Berkeley EECS and BART do for Women’s History Month?
chunks.answer: Worked together to Embrace Equity in STEM.

chunks.text: February 21, 2023: The Black Engineering and Science Student Association (BESSA) and Black Graduate Engineering and Science Students (BGESS) celebrated their 55th and 35th anniversaries, respectively. The event on February 11, 2023, was hosted by the Black Engineering and Science Alumni Club (BESAC).
chunks.question: Which organizations celebrated milestone anniversaries in February 2023?

chunks.text: February 3, 2023: Hannah Joo, an undergraduate student studying computer science and cognitive science at Berkeley, has won a Brooke Owens Fellowship. She will receive internships, mentorships, and a professional network along with 47 other undergraduate women and gender minorities worldwide.
chunks.question: What did Hannah Joo receive as a Brooke Owens Fellow?
chunks.answer: Internships, mentorships, and a professional network.

chunks.text: December 21, 2022: The EECS department is recognizing Berkeley EE Prof. Joseph T. Gier, the University of California’s first tenured Black professor, with a memorial sculpture. Raised in Oakland by a single mother, Gier came to Berkeley and made significant contributions.
chunks.question: Who is being honored with a memorial sculpture by Berkeley EECS?

chunks.text: September 29, 2022: CS Prof. Raluca Ada Popa was interviewed as a Featured ACM Member in the 'People of ACM' bulletin. As Co-Director of RISELab and SkyLab, she builds secure intelligent systems for the cloud and sky.
chunks.question: What labs does Raluca Ada Popa co-direct?

chunks.text: September 22, 2022: EECS Prof. Emerita Ruzena Bajcsy has been awarded the Slovak Medal of Honor for her scientific achievements and positive representation of Slovakia abroad. The medal was presented by Slovak President Zuzana Čaputová.
chunks.question: What award did Ruzena Bajcsy receive in September 2022?

chunks.text: August 31, 2022: CS Kickstart held its one-week computer science immersion program in its 11th year, with over 95 attendees, a record turnout. The program introduces female-identifying first-year students to computer science at Berkeley to increase diversity.
chunks.question: What is the goal of the CS Kickstart program?
chunks.answer: To increase diversity in computer science.
